answer: In 'Hai baske har ek unke ishaare mein nishaan aur' (Diwan-E-Ghalib, chapter 60), Ghalib says that every single gesture of the beloved contains yet another hidden sign within it ('nishaan aur' — another mark/sign). No gesture is simple or singular; each one is layered, carrying a further concealed indication beyond what appears on the surface. The opening couplet pairs this with the observation that when the beloved does show love, it only breeds yet another kind of suspicion or doubt ('guzarta hai gumaan aur'), so the beloved's gestures are endlessly complex — always pointing beyond themselves, never resolving into certainty. The poem thus presents the beloved's every movement as an inexhaustible, multiplying system of signs that the lover can never fully decode.
